evidence: 7 packages used for 30 production files: api(6), config(4), exploration(2), indexer/blacklab(2), model(7), query(5), utils(3), viz(1) — counts are well-proportioned and no single directory overloads.
PatternSubstitution.java lives in api/ but has zero HTTP imports; it imports only java.util.List and utils.CqlUtils and performs CQL token manipulation — a utils concern.
CqlUtils.java (in utils/) documents its relationship with PatternSubstitution by full package path in Javadoc, signalling the two belong together.
PatternSubstitutionTest.java lives in src/test/.../utils/ (the utils test package), not in the api test package, confirming tester intent that this is a utils-layer class.
RelationUtils.java is package-private in api/ and used only within the api package — a correct placement that maps user-facing HTTP params to grammar config IDs.
viz/ is a single-file package (RadialPlot.java only), referenced only from SketchHandlers.java in api/. The concern is legitimate but the package is thin.

strengths: Package names (api, config, exploration, indexer/blacklab, model, query, utils, viz) directly reflect functional responsibilities — a developer can predict which package owns a given concern without inspecting file contents.
No directory overload: the largest packages (model at 7 files, api at 6) are still well within the 10-file flat-overload threshold and contain cohesive sets of files.
Dependency direction is generally clean: exploration imports from model/query/config, api imports from exploration/model/query/config — no upstream-to-downstream import reversals.
indexer/blacklab correctly namespaces the BlackLab-specific implementation under a subdirectory, leaving room for alternative indexer implementations.
package_organization.issue_character: The single placement defect (PatternSubstitution mishoused in api/) is an isolated, localized mismatch rather than a systemic pattern — the rest of the structure is coherent.
package_organization.score_rationale: The codebase is at the 80–90 anchor range: a developer navigating it would correctly predict ownership for almost every file based on directory names alone, with one recurring surprise — PatternSubstitution sits in api/ despite being a CQL utility with no HTTP surface. That single misplacement is confirmed by three independent signals (no HTTP imports, CqlUtils Javadoc cross-reference, and test file in the utils test package), which pulls the score below 90. The viz/ single-file package is thin but serves a clear, distinct concern and is not a defect.

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution.java lives in api/ but is a CQL utility with no HTTP surface
evidence: PatternSubstitution.java imports only java.util.List and pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils.CqlUtils — no com.sun.net.httpserver or HTTP-related types anywhere in the file.
CqlUtils.java Javadoc (line 13) names PatternSubstitution by its full api-package path as a related class, indicating the author treats them as peers.
PatternSubstitutionTest.java is placed under src/test/.../utils/, not under .../api/, confirming the test author considers it a utils-layer concern.
PatternSubstitution is used exclusively by SketchHandlers.java (api/), but the calling site is routing glue — the CQL manipulation logic itself has no dependency on the HTTP layer.
issues.suggestion: Move PatternSubstitution.java to src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/utils/. Steps: (1) update package declaration to pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils; (2) update the import in SketchHandlers.java from api.PatternSubstitution to utils.PatternSubstitution; (3) update the import in PatternSubstitutionTest.java; (4) verify with mvn test -Dtest=PatternSubstitutionTest. No logic changes required.

root_causes: PatternSubstitution was likely created as a direct extraction from SketchHandlers.java, inheriting the handler's api/ package by default, without a subsequent move to utils/ where its dependency footprint places it.
